title: Ascension, Spirit promise, Matthias
summary_text: Writing again to Theophilus, Luke recounts Jesus’s post-resurrection teaching on the Kingdom, the command to stay in Jerusalem for the Father’s promise, the Spirit baptism coming soon, and the commission to witness to the ends of the earth. Jesus ascends in a cloud; two figures promise his return. The believers pray together; Peter applies Scripture to Judas, and Matthias is chosen by lot to join the eleven.
key_points: Jesus showed himself alive forty days and spoke about God’s Kingdom.
Apostles were told to wait in Jerusalem for the Father’s promise.
They would receive power when the Holy Spirit came and be witnesses everywhere.
Jesus was taken up in a cloud; two men said he would return the same way.
Peter said Scripture required Judas’s office to pass to another.
A successor must have been with them from John’s baptism to the ascension.
They drew lots and Matthias was numbered with the eleven apostles.
evidence_phrases: you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit
You will be witnesses to me
a cloud received him out of their sight
will come back in the same way
Let another take his office
the lot fell on Matthias

title: Pentecost, Peter’s sermon, three thousand
summary_text: On Pentecost the believers are filled with the Holy Spirit and speak other languages as devout Jews from many nations hear God’s mighty works in their own tongues. Peter denies drunkenness and quotes Joel, then proclaims Jesus approved by God, crucified yet raised, exalted to pour out the Spirit. Cut to the heart, hearers ask what to do; Peter calls for repentance, baptism in Jesus’s name, and the gift of the Spirit. About three thousand are baptized and devote themselves to the apostles’ teaching, fellowship, breaking bread, and prayer, with signs and shared possessions.
key_points: A sound like wind filled the house; tongues like fire rested on each.
They spoke in languages the Spirit gave them.
Peter said this fulfilled Joel’s outpouring on all flesh.
He declared Jesus crucified by lawless hands but raised by God.
God made Jesus both Lord and Christ.
About three thousand were baptized that day.
The Lord added to the assembly daily those being saved.
evidence_phrases: filled with the Holy Spirit
this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel
God raised up
God has made him both Lord and Christ
Repent, and be baptized
about three thousand souls
The Lord added to the assembly

title: Lame man healed, Solomon’s Porch sermon
summary_text: Peter and John heal a man lame from birth at the Beautiful Gate in Jesus’s name; he walks and praises God. In Solomon’s porch Peter denies their own power, says the God of the fathers glorified his Servant Jesus whom they delivered up and killed, and that faith in Jesus’s name made the man whole. He urges repentance for sins to be blotted out and times of refreshing, cites Moses on the prophet to hear and Abraham’s blessing to all families, and says God raised Jesus to bless them by turning them from wickedness.
themes: Silver and gold I have not
key_points: Peter commanded the beggar to walk in the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth.
The man leaped and walked into the temple praising God.
Peter said they denied the Holy and Righteous One before Pilate.
Faith in Jesus’s name gave the man perfect soundness.
He called for repentance so sins might be blotted out.
He cited Moses about the prophet they must hear.
God sent Jesus first to bless by turning them from wickedness.
evidence_phrases: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk
Silver and gold I have not
whom you delivered up, and denied in the presence of Pilate
killed the Prince of life, whom God raised from the dead
Repent therefore, and turn again
All the families of the earth will be blessed

title: Sanhedrin threat, prayer, Barnabas
summary_text: Priests and Sadducees arrest Peter and John for teaching resurrection in Jesus; many believe, reaching about five thousand men. Before rulers Peter declares the crippled man healed in Jesus’s name, crucified and raised—the stone rejected, the only name for salvation. The council marvels at unlearned boldness and the healed man, threaten silence, but Peter and John say they must speak what they saw and heard. The church prays for boldness amid threats; the place shakes and they are filled with the Spirit. Believers share possessions; Barnabas sells a field for the apostles.
key_points: They were seized for proclaiming resurrection in Jesus.
Peter said the man stands whole in the name of Jesus raised from the dead.
There is no other name by which we must be saved.
Peter and John refused to stop speaking what they witnessed.
The company prayed for boldness despite threats.
The place was shaken; they spoke God’s word with boldness.
Barnabas sold a field and laid the money at the apostles’ feet.
evidence_phrases: proclaimed in Jesus the resurrection from the dead
whom you crucified, whom God raised from the dead
There is salvation in no one else
We must obey God rather than men
the place was shaken
of one heart and soul
Joses, who by the apostles was also called Barnabas

title: Ananias and Sapphira, prison break, Gamaliel
summary_text: Ananias and Sapphira fall dead for lying to the Holy Spirit about their sale price. Great fear attends signs at Solomon’s porch; multitudes are healed. The high priest’s party jails the apostles, but an angel opens the prison and sends them to teach in the temple. Brought before the council, Peter says we must obey God: God raised Jesus as Prince and Savior. Gamaliel urges caution, comparing past failed movements; the apostles are beaten, rejoice in dishonor for Jesus’s name, and keep teaching daily.
key_points: Peter charged Ananias with lying to the Holy Spirit, not merely men.
Sapphira fell dead after agreeing to the lie.
An angel freed the apostles to speak in the temple.
Peter said God exalted Jesus to give repentance and forgiveness to Israel.
Gamaliel warned against fighting God if the work is of him.
The apostles were beaten yet rejoiced to suffer for Jesus’s name.
They did not stop teaching and preaching Jesus the Christ.
evidence_phrases: You haven’t lied to men, but to God
Go stand and speak in the temple
We must obey God rather than men
fighting against God
rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer dishonor

title: Seven chosen, Stephen accused
summary_text: Hellenist widows’ neglect leads the twelve to appoint seven Spirit-filled men for table service while they give themselves to word and prayer. Stephen, full of faith and power, does wonders; opponents dispute him, suborn false witnesses about Moses and the holy place, and drag him to the council where his face shines like an angel’s.
key_points: Complaint arose that Hellenist widows were neglected.
Seven men full of the Spirit and wisdom were chosen and hands laid on them.
The word multiplied; many priests obeyed the faith.
Stephen did great wonders among the people.
False witnesses said he spoke against the holy place and Moses’s customs.
The council saw his face like an angel’s face.
evidence_phrases: It is not appropriate for us to forsake the word of God and serve tables
Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit
The word of God increased
They weren’t able to withstand the wisdom and the Spirit
his face like it was the face of an angel

title: Stephen’s speech and stoning
summary_text: The high priest asks if charges are true; Stephen recounts God’s dealings with Abraham, Joseph, Moses, the exodus, the golden calf, the tabernacle, David, Solomon, and the prophets—charging the council with resisting the Holy Spirit as their fathers did, persecuting prophets, and betraying the Righteous One. He sees heaven opened and the Son of Man at God’s right hand; they stone him as he prays Jesus receive his spirit and not hold the sin against them. Witnesses lay garments at Saul’s feet.
themes: Son of Man at God’s right
key_points: Stephen traced Israel’s history from Abraham through Moses and the prophets.
He accused them of always resisting the Holy Spirit.
He said they betrayed and murdered the Righteous One.
He saw the Son of Man standing at God’s right hand.
They stoned him outside the city.
He asked the Lord Jesus to receive his spirit.
He asked that their sin not be held against them.
evidence_phrases: You stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart and ears
I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing
Lord Jesus, receive my spirit
don’t hold this sin against them
the witnesses placed their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul

title: Persecution, Philip, Simon, Ethiopian eunuch
summary_text: Saul consents to Stephen’s death; persecution scatters all but apostles; Saul ravages the church. Scattered believers preach the word. Philip evangelizes Samaria with signs; Simon believes but tries to buy the Spirit’s gift and is rebuked. An angel sends Philip to the Gaza road; he explains Isaiah’s suffering servant to an Ethiopian official, baptizes him at water, and is caught away to Azotus, preaching to Caesarea.
key_points: Saul consented to Stephen’s death and later dragged believers to prison.
Those scattered preached the word.
Philip proclaimed Christ in Samaria with deliverances and healings.
Peter and John prayed and laid hands so Samaritans received the Holy Spirit.
Peter told Simon his money should perish for seeking to buy God’s gift.
Philip baptized the eunuch after preaching Jesus from Isaiah.
The Spirit caught Philip away; he preached to cities as far as Caesarea.
evidence_phrases: Saul was consenting to his death
Philip went down to the city of Samaria
May your silver perish with you
they received the Holy Spirit
Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture, preached to him about Jesus
the Spirit of the Lord caught Philip away

title: Saul’s conversion, Peter’s healings
summary_text: Saul, breathing threats, seeks Damascus letters; the risen Lord blinds him and calls him chosen vessel to bear Christ’s name to nations, kings, and Israel. Ananias restores sight and fills him with the Spirit; Saul preaches Christ in Damascus, escapes in a basket, and Barnabas introduces him in Jerusalem. Churches have peace; Peter heals Aeneas at Lydda and raises Tabitha (Dorcas) at Joppa, then stays with Simon the tanner.
key_points: Jesus identified himself as the one Saul persecuted.
Ananias laid hands so Saul received sight and the Holy Spirit.
Saul immediately proclaimed Jesus as the Son of God in synagogues.
Disciples lowered him in a basket when Jews plotted to kill him.
Barnabas told the apostles how Saul had seen the Lord.
Peter healed paralyzed Aeneas in Lydda.
Peter raised Tabitha, and many believed in Joppa.
evidence_phrases: Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?
I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting
he is my chosen vessel
lowered him in a basket
Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles
Jesus Christ heals you. Get up and make your bed
Tabitha, get up!

title: Cornelius, Peter’s vision, Gentiles receive the Spirit
summary_text: Devout centurion Cornelius is told by an angel to send for Peter in Joppa. Peter’s trance teaches not to call unclean what God cleansed. At Caesarea Peter preaches Jesus anointed with the Spirit, crucified, raised, and appointed judge; while he speaks the Holy Spirit falls on the Gentiles as on Pentecost. Peter orders their baptism in Jesus’s name.
key_points: An angel told Cornelius to send for Peter at Joppa.
Peter saw the sheet vision three times with the command to eat.
Peter said God showed him not to call any man unholy or unclean.
He testified that God accepts those who fear him and work righteousness.
The Holy Spirit fell on hearers before baptism was finished.
They spoke in languages and magnified God.
Peter commanded baptism in the name of Jesus Christ.
evidence_phrases: Your prayers and your gifts to the needy have gone up for a memorial
What God has cleansed, you must not call unclean
God doesn’t show favoritism
the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the word
They have received the Holy Spirit just like us

title: Peter’s defense, Antioch, Christians, famine relief
summary_text: Circumcision believers challenge Peter; he recounts the vision, the Spirit’s command to go, and the Spirit falling as at first, asking who could withstand God. They glorify God for Gentile repentance. Scattered believers reach Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch; some preach to Hellenists at Antioch. Barnabas encourages the church, fetches Saul, and they teach a year—the disciples are first called Christians. Agabus prophesies famine; the church sends relief by Barnabas and Saul to Judean elders.
key_points: Peter retold the sheet vision and Spirit falling on Gentiles.
The Jerusalem group glorified God for Gentile repentance to life.
A great number believed at Antioch.
Disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.
Agabus foretold a great famine in Claudius’s days.
Disciples sent relief to Judea by Barnabas and Saul.
evidence_phrases: God gave to them the same gift as us
God has also granted to the Gentiles repentance to life
The disciples were first called Christians in Antioch
there should be a great famine all over the world

title: James killed, Peter freed, Herod struck
summary_text: Herod kills James and imprisons Peter during Unleavened Bread; prayer is made for Peter. An angel frees Peter from chains and the iron gate; Rhoda’s joy delays the door; Peter reports to James and the brothers then leaves. Herod executes guards, accepts Tyre and Sidon’s flattery, is struck by an angel for not giving God glory, and dies eaten by worms. God’s word grows; Barnabas and Saul return with John Mark.
key_points: Herod killed James with the sword and seized Peter.
An angel woke Peter, chains fell, and the iron gate opened.
Rhoda recognized Peter’s voice but the house doubted.
Herod had the guards put to death when Peter vanished.
An angel struck Herod for accepting divine honors; worms consumed him.
Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem with John Mark.
evidence_phrases: constant prayer was made by the assembly to God for him
His chains fell off his hands
Now I truly know that the Lord has sent out his angel
The voice of a god, and not of a man
he was eaten by worms and died
the word of God grew and multiplied

title: First missionary journey begins
summary_text: At Antioch the Holy Spirit sets apart Barnabas and Saul; after fasting and laying on of hands they sail to Cyprus, confound Elymas at Paphos where Sergius Paulus believes, and John Mark returns to Jerusalem. At Pisidian Antioch Paul preaches Israel’s history, Jesus crucified and raised, forgiveness through him, and warns from Habakkuk. Jews reject; Paul turns to Gentiles per Isaiah; believers rejoice amid persecution as Paul and Barnabas go to Iconium.
key_points: The Spirit said to separate Barnabas and Saul for his work.
Paul struck Elymas blind for opposing the faith.
Paul preached Jesus crucified, buried, raised, and seen by witnesses.
He offered remission of sins and justification not by Moses’s law alone.
Jews contradicted; Paul said they judged themselves unworthy of eternal life.
Gentiles glorified the word; as many as appointed to eternal life believed.
evidence_phrases: Separate Barnabas and Saul for me
Saul, who is also called Paul
the hand of the Lord is on you, and you will be blind
God has fulfilled this to us in that he raised up Jesus
we turn to the Gentiles
as many as were appointed to eternal life believed

title: Iconium, Lystra, gods mistaken, return with elders
summary_text: In Iconium Paul and Barnabas speak boldly with signs amid division and flee a stoning plot to Lycaonia. At Lystra Paul heals a cripple; crowds call them Jupiter and Mercury until the apostles tear their clothes and preach the living Creator. Jews from Antioch and Iconium stone Paul; he rises and goes to Derbe. They return appointing elders in every church, report God opening faith to nations, and stay long at Antioch.
key_points: The Lord testified to his word with signs in Iconium.
Paul healed a lifelong cripple who had faith to be healed.
They urged Lycaonians to turn from vain things to the living God.
Jews persuaded the crowd to stone Paul; he entered the city next day.
They appointed elders with prayer and fasting in each assembly.
They reported God had opened a door of faith to the nations.
evidence_phrases: the Lord, who testified to the word of his grace
Stand upright on your feet!
We also are men of like passions with you
through many afflictions we must enter into God’s Kingdom
he had opened a door of faith to the nations

title: Jerusalem council, letter, Paul and Silas
summary_text: Men from Judea say Gentiles must be circumcised to be saved; Paul and Barnabas dispute and go to Jerusalem. Peter recalls Cornelius: God gave the Spirit without distinction, cleansing hearts by faith; James cites Amos on Gentiles called by God’s name and urges letter on abstaining from idol pollution, sexual immorality, strangled meat, and blood. Judas Barsabbas and Silas deliver the letter; after sharp words over Mark, Barnabas sails to Cyprus with Mark while Paul takes Silas through Syria and Cilicia.
key_points: Some said Gentiles must be circumcised after Moses to be saved.
Peter said God gave Gentiles the Holy Spirit as to Jewish believers.
James judged not to trouble Gentile converts beyond four abstentions.
The letter said they gave no command to circumcise and keep the whole law.
It seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to the apostles and elders.
Paul and Barnabas separated over taking Mark; Paul went with Silas.
evidence_phrases: cleansing their hearts by faith
we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus
It seemed good to the Holy Spirit, and to us
abstain from things sacrificed to idols, from blood
Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus

title: Timothy, Macedonia call, Philippian jail
summary_text: Paul takes Timothy, circumcised for Jews’ sake, and delivers the Jerusalem decrees. The Spirit blocks Asia and Bithynia; a Macedonian vision sends them to Philippi. Lydia believes; Paul casts out a spirit of divination, is beaten and jailed, but an earthquake opens doors; the jailer asks how to be saved, believes with his household, and Paul reveals Roman citizenship before leaving Lydia’s house.
key_points: Paul circumcised Timothy because Jews knew his father was Greek.
They delivered the apostles’ and elders’ decrees to strengthen churches.
Paul saw a man of Macedonia begging help in a vision.
The Lord opened Lydia’s heart to Paul’s words.
Paul commanded an unclean spirit out in Jesus’s name.
The jailer and household were baptized after believing.
Magistrates begged Paul and Silas when they learned they were Romans.
evidence_phrases: Come over into Macedonia and help us
The Lord opened her heart
I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!
Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, and you will be saved
They have beaten us publicly without a trial, men who are Romans

title: Thessalonica, Beroea, Athens Areopagus
summary_text: Paul reasons in Thessalonica’s synagogue that Christ must suffer and rise; some Jews stir a mob against Jason. In Beroea Jews search Scripture daily; Thessalonian Jews pursue Paul to the sea. In Athens Paul argues in synagogue and marketplace, is taken to the Areopagus, proclaims the unknown God as Creator who commands repentance and will judge the world by the man raised from the dead. Some mock; others want to hear more; Dionysius, Damaris, and others believe.
key_points: Paul showed from Scripture that Christ must suffer and rise.
Accusers charged Jason with welcoming those who say another king, Jesus.
Beroeans examined the Scriptures daily.
Paul cited the altar TO AN UNKNOWN GOD.
He said God commands all people everywhere to repent.
He set a day of judgment by the man God raised from the dead.
evidence_phrases: This Jesus, whom I proclaim to you, is the Christ
examining the Scriptures daily to see whether these things were so
TO AN UNKNOWN GOD
he has appointed a day in which he will judge the world
raised him from the dead

title: Corinth, Gallio, Apollos
summary_text: Paul joins tentmakers Aquila and Priscilla in Corinth, teaches in the synagogue until opposition, then stays with Justus next door; Crispus and many believe. The Lord encourages Paul in a vision not to fear harm. Gallio dismisses the Jewish case as words and law. Paul sails with Priscilla and Aquila, stops briefly at Ephesus, vows at Cenchreae, greets Jerusalem and Antioch, then strengthens Galatia and Phrygia. Apollos, knowing only John’s baptism, is instructed more accurately by Priscilla and Aquila and proves Jesus is the Christ in Achaia.
themes: I will go to the Gentiles
key_points: Paul reasoned in Corinth’s synagogue every Sabbath.
The Lord told Paul not to fear; he had many people in the city.
Gallio refused to judge questions of Jewish law.
Paul left Priscilla and Aquila at Ephesus.
Apollos knew only John’s baptism until Priscilla and Aquila taught him.
Apollos publicly showed from Scripture that Jesus was the Christ.
evidence_phrases: I am with you, and no one will attack you to harm you
From now on, I will go to the Gentiles!
I don’t want to be a judge of these matters
explained to him the way of God more accurately
publicly showing by the Scriptures that Jesus was the Christ

title: Ephesus: Spirit, school, riot
summary_text: Paul finds disciples baptized only with John’s baptism; they are baptized in Jesus’s name, receive the Spirit with tongues and prophecy. He teaches boldly in the synagogue then two years in Tyrannus’s school so all Asia hears. Unusual miracles through Paul include handkerchiefs; sons of Sceva are beaten by an evil spirit. Many confess and burn magic books. Paul resolves to see Rome after Jerusalem. Demetrius the silversmith stirs a riot over Artemis; the town clerk calms the crowd about Gaius and Aristarchus.
key_points: Twelve men were re-baptized and received the Holy Spirit through Paul’s hands.
Paul taught daily two years; Asia heard the word.
Evil spirit knew Jesus and Paul but not the exorcists.
Believers burned books worth fifty thousand pieces of silver.
Paul said he must see Rome after Jerusalem.
Demetrius feared Paul’s teaching hurt the Artemis trade.
The town clerk warned against riot without cause.
evidence_phrases: we haven’t even heard that there is a Holy Spirit
reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus
Jesus I know, and Paul I know, but who are you?
fifty thousand pieces of silver
Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!

title: Miletus farewell to elders
summary_text: After the riot Paul encourages disciples, tours Macedonia and Greece, and sails from Philippi. At Troas Eutychus falls from a third story; Paul embraces him and he lives. Paul bypasses Ephesus and calls elders to Miletus, recalling his humble teaching, tears, and call to repentance and faith. He warns of wolves and perverse speakers, commends them to God and grace, cites giving more than receiving, prays, and they weep at his face they will see no more.
key_points: Paul raised Eutychus after he fell dead from the window.
He said he declared the whole counsel of God publicly and house to house.
He testified to repentance toward God and faith toward Jesus.
He warned overseers to shepherd the church God purchased with his own blood.
He foretold grievous wolves and men speaking perverse things.
He quoted Jesus: it is more blessed to give than to receive.
evidence_phrases: his life is in him
I didn’t shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God
to shepherd the assembly of the Lord and God which he purchased with his own blood
vicious wolves will enter in among you
It is more blessed to give than to receive

title: Journey to Jerusalem, temple uproar
summary_text: Paul sails through Tyre and Caesarea; disciples and Agabus warn against Jerusalem via the Spirit, but Paul is ready to die for Jesus’s name. In Jerusalem he reports to James and elders; they ask him to purify with four men under vow to refute rumors. Jews from Asia seize him in the temple over Trophimus; the crowd tries to kill him until the Roman commander rescues and binds him. Paul speaks Greek, identifies as Tarsus, and is allowed to address the people in Hebrew.
themes: James and thousands zealous for law
key_points: Tyre disciples said through the Spirit not to go up to Jerusalem.
Agabus bound Paul’s belt symbolizing binding at Jerusalem.
Paul said he was ready to die for the Lord Jesus’s name.
Elders asked Paul to join purification to show he kept the law.
Jews falsely accused him of bringing Trophimus into the temple.
The commander arrested Paul and bound him with chains.
Paul asked to speak to the crowd in Hebrew.
evidence_phrases: through the Spirit that he should not go up to Jerusalem
I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die
The Lord’s will be done
purify yourself with them
he also brought Greeks into the temple
I am a Jew, from Tarsus in Cilicia

title: Paul’s defense to the crowd
summary_text: Paul tells the Jerusalem crowd in Hebrew of his Gamaliel training, zeal persecuting the Way, Damascus light and Jesus’s voice, Ananias restoring sight and commissioning witness, and the temple trance sending him to the Gentiles. The crowd erupts until the commander orders scourging; Paul claims Roman citizenship born, so examination stops. Next day he is brought before the council.
key_points: Paul said he was educated under Gamaliel and persecuted the Way.
Jesus identified himself on the Damascus road.
Ananias told him to be baptized and wash away sins.
The Lord sent him far to the Gentiles.
The crowd raged when he mentioned Gentiles.
Paul’s Roman birth halted illegal scourging.
evidence_phrases: I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you persecute
Arise, and go into Damascus
wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord
I will send you out far from here to the Gentiles
But I was born a Roman

title: Sanhedrin split, plot foiled, Felix
summary_text: Paul claims good conscience; high priest Ananias orders a strike; Paul apologizes unknowingly insulting the ruler. He divides Pharisees and Sadducees over resurrection hope. A night vision assures Paul he will testify at Rome. Forty Jews vow to kill Paul; his nephew warns the commander, who sends Paul with heavy escort and a letter to Felix at Caesarea.
key_points: Paul said he lived in good conscience before God.
He split the council by professing hope in the resurrection.
The Lord said Paul would testify at Rome as at Jerusalem.
More than forty men vowed not to eat until they killed Paul.
Paul’s nephew told the commander the ambush plan.
Paul was sent by night with soldiers to Felix in Caesarea.
evidence_phrases: Concerning the hope and resurrection of the dead I am being judged
Cheer up, Paul, for as you have testified about me at Jerusalem, so you must testify also at Rome
neither eat nor drink until they have killed Paul
Claudius Lysias to the most excellent governor Felix

title: Trial before Felix
summary_text: Tertullus accuses Paul before Felix as a plague, sect ringleader, and temple profaner. Paul denies stirring crowds, confesses serving the God of fathers in the Way, hopes in resurrection of just and unjust, and came to bring alms. Felix defers until Lysias comes, keeps Paul with privileges, hears him with Drusilla on faith, trembles at judgment talk, hopes for a bribe, and leaves Paul bound two years until Festus succeeds.
key_points: Tertullus called Paul a ringleader of the Nazarene sect.
Paul confessed worshiping the God of the fathers according to the Way.
He said he believed in resurrection of both just and unjust.
Felix postponed judgment wanting Lysias’s account.
Felix trembled when Paul spoke of righteousness and coming judgment.
Felix left Paul bound two years to please the Jews.
evidence_phrases: a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes
I serve the God of our fathers, believing all things which are according to the law
there will be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust
Felix was terrified
Felix left Paul in bonds

title: Festus and appeal to Caesar
summary_text: Festus refuses to send Paul to Jerusalem for ambush; at Caesarea Paul denies sin against law, temple, or Caesar. Festus favors Jews; Paul appeals to Caesar. Agrippa and Bernice visit; Festus explains the case is about their religion and a dead Jesus Paul says is alive. Agrippa hears Paul next day.
key_points: Jews asked Festus to summon Paul to Jerusalem to kill him en route.
Paul said he stood before Caesar’s seat where he ought be tried.
Paul appealed to Caesar; Festus granted it.
Festus told Agrippa the dispute was about Jesus dead vs. alive.
Agrippa wanted to hear Paul himself.
evidence_phrases: plotting to kill him on the way
I am standing before Caesar’s judgment seat
I appeal to Caesar!
one Jesus, who was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive
Tomorrow you shall hear him

title: Before Agrippa
summary_text: Paul tells Agrippa he is judged for hope in the patriarchs’ promise and resurrection. He recounts persecuting saints, Damascus light, Jesus’s commission to open Gentile eyes to repentance and forgiveness, and his obedience preaching repentance. Festus calls him mad; Paul appeals to truth and Agrippa’s knowledge. Agrippa asks if Paul would make him a Christian; rulers find no death-worthy crime; Agrippa says Paul could have been freed without appealing to Caesar.
key_points: Paul said he was a Pharisee judged for hope of resurrection.
Jesus commissioned him to turn Gentiles from darkness to light.
Festus said Paul’s learning made him insane.
Paul said he spoke true and reasonable words.
Agrippa asked if Paul tried to make him a Christian.
The king and governor agreed Paul did nothing worthy of death or chains.
evidence_phrases: for the hope of the promise made by God to our fathers
to open their eyes, that they may turn from darkness to light
I am not crazy, most excellent Festus
With a little persuasion are you trying to make me a Christian?
This man might have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar

title: Storm and shipwreck
summary_text: Paul and prisoners sail under Julius; contrary winds delay them. Paul warns sailing from Fair Havens will bring loss and danger; the centurion heeds the ship’s master over Paul. Euroclydon drives them; cargo and tackle are cast overboard. An angel tells Paul all aboard will be saved though the ship is lost; Paul urges food after fourteen days fasting. They run aground at Malta; soldiers’ plan to kill prisoners is stopped by Julius; all reach shore safely.
key_points: Paul foresaw injury and loss if they sailed from Crete.
The ship was caught by Euroclydon and driven days without sun or stars.
An angel said Paul must stand before Caesar and all would be saved.
Paul said not a hair would perish from their heads.
Two hundred seventy-six people were on the ship.
Julius kept soldiers from killing prisoners so Paul could be saved.
evidence_phrases: the voyage will be with injury and much loss
which is called Euroclydon
all hope that we would be saved was now taken away
You must stand before Caesar
two hundred seventy-six souls on the ship
So they all escaped safely to the land

title: Malta, Rome, Isaiah, open preaching
summary_text: On Malta Paul is unharmed by a viper; natives first call him murderer then a god. He heals Publius’s father and many sick. After three months they sail to Rome via Syracuse and Puteoli; brothers encourage Paul on the road. Under guard he calls Jewish leaders, explains his appeal for Israel’s hope, and testifies to the Kingdom and Jesus from Moses and prophets all day. Some believe, some not; he cites Isaiah on hardened hearts and says salvation is sent to the nations. Two years he teaches boldly unhindered in his rented lodging.
key_points: Paul shook off the viper into the fire unharmed.
He healed Publius’s father of fever and dysentery.
In Rome he said he was bound for the hope of Israel.
He reasoned about God’s Kingdom and Jesus from law and prophets.
He applied Isaiah about dull hearing and closed eyes.
He said God’s salvation was sent to the nations who would listen.
Two years he preached God’s Kingdom and taught about Jesus boldly.
evidence_phrases: he shook off the creature into the fire, and wasn’t harmed
because of the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain
persuading them concerning Jesus, both from the law of Moses and from the prophets
the salvation of God is sent to the nations
preaching God’s Kingdom, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, without hindrance
